summary: Full backend + frontend regression for cookie-auth migration. All 14 backend pytest cases passed. Frontend admin login/logout/session-persistence/CRUD verified. httpOnly access_token cookie confirmed (secure=True, sameSite=Lax). localStorage.ips_admin_token is null (no token in JS-accessible storage). Public admission form submission works. All public pages return 200. Home split-components render correctly (hero, news ticker, stats visible).

issues: Console shows 2 x 401 on every page load from /api/auth/me hydration call. Not a functional bug (AuthContext expected behavior for anonymous users) but produces console noise — consider silencing 401 in axios interceptor for /auth/me only, or short-circuiting when no cookie present.

action_items: (Optional) Suppress 401 console errors on public pages by handling /api/auth/me 401 silently in AuthContext (don't throw / avoid console.error).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 400 lines — approaching split threshold; consider breaking into routers/auth.py, routers/admin.py, routers/public.py in future iterations.
COOKIE_SECURE=true works because preview domain is HTTPS; ensure this stays true in prod. Good.
CORS explicit origins from env — correct with allow_credentials=True. Good.
No rate-limiting / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook mentions lockout after 5 fails). Not blocking, but flag for future hardening.
get_current_admin returns 401 for both unauthenticated and forbidden (non-admin) — semantically the latter should be 403. Minor.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_UI_News item and one TEST_UI_Parent admission during UI test; harmless test data prefixed with TEST_
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cookie-based auth verified: login sets httpOnly+secure+SameSite=Lax access_token cookie, /api/auth/me works with cookie only (no Authorization header) and with Bearer fallback. Logout clears cookie. Session survives hard reload via AuthContext hydration. No token in localStorage. Public admission form uses native <select> elements — use page.select_option instead of page.fill for input-class/input-branch.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Only observation: 401 responses from /api/auth/me on public pages generate console errors (cosmetic).
